applies_when: "A real-life customer appears as two different accounts (often from POS vs online)"
---

# Customer Account Issues

## Cause
Shopify creates two separate customer IDs when identifying info doesn't match exactly - e.g. when a customer buys in-store via POS without using the same email/phone as their online account. Joy then sees them as two distinct customers.

## Resolution Steps
- **Step 1:** Verify with merchant that the two accounts are the same person; collect both emails.
- **Step 2:** Explain to merchant that Shopify creates separate IDs when contact info differs, so Joy reflects two users.
- **Step 3:** Suggest merging the two accounts. If merchant agrees, escalate to team to perform the merge and append `<escalate_human>` to the reply. After merging, Joy will sync points/tier into the single remaining account.

## Notes
- CS does not merge accounts directly in Joy - it depends on Shopify customer merge.
- Always explain how Shopify and Joy identify customers to avoid confusion.

## Related
- kb_customers

---

## Cause
- After install, all customers should sync from Shopify to Joy automatically. Sync may have failed for some.

## Resolution Steps
- **Step 1:** Ask merchant for their Shopify customer count.
- **Step 2:** If count mismatch persists after checking, escalate to team with the store URL to trigger a customer sync — append `<escalate_human>` to the reply. Bot stops here — team handles the rest.

## Related
- kb_customers

---

## Cause
The **Export customers** button needs to be enabled by the team before it appears on the Customers page.

## Resolution Steps
- **Step 1:** Ask merchant for their store URL.
- **Step 2:** Tell the merchant our team will enable the Export customers feature for them.
- **Step 3:** Escalate to team to enable the export feature — append `<escalate_human>` to the reply. Bot stops here — team handles the rest.

## Related
- kb_customers

---

## Resolution Steps
- **Step 1:** Collect the store URL and a sample customer name that fails to be found via search.
- **Step 2:** Tell the merchant our team will fix the search issue for them.
- **Step 3:** Escalate to team to fix the search issue — append `<escalate_human>` to the reply. Bot stops here — team handles the rest.

## Related
- kb_customers

---

## Customer Accounts (new)

A customer added in-store (**POS**) reaches the same loyalty account online by logging in with the **same email** — with New Customer Accounts they use the email / one-time-code flow (no password to set). In-store and online activity tie to the same email and the same points balance.

- Shopify uses single sign-on (email-only login). Anyone who enters their email at checkout or for newsletter is auto-created with status **Enabled**, and Joy treats an "Enabled" customer as a **Member**.
- **However, being a Member is not the same as earning the Sign up reward.** Leaving an email alone (signup form, checkout, or newsletter) no longer grants the Sign up reward.

## Sign up reward — when it is actually granted (ALL account types)
A customer only truly earns the **Sign up** reward when **both** conditions are met:
1. The customer **logs in to a customer account** on the store, AND
2. The **Joy embed widget is active/enabled** on the storefront (Theme Editor → App embeds → Joy Loyalty - Widget → Save).

If the widget is turned off, the Sign up reward will not trigger even when the customer logs in. This applies to New Customer Accounts and Legacy Accounts alike.

## Legacy (classic) Accounts
- **Member**: customer who actively created an account with a password.
- **Guest**: customer who placed an order or signed up without creating an account.
- Joy still distinguishes the two for classification, but the Sign up reward condition above (login + active widget) applies to all account types.

## How to Decide
- The Sign up reward fires the same way regardless of account type: the customer must log in and the Joy embed widget must be enabled.
- Choose account type based on the login experience you want, not on how signup points are granted: Legacy = email/password with richer profiles; New = email-link (one-time-code) login with lower friction.
- If a merchant reports signup points missing, first confirm the customer logged in and the embed widget is enabled — see kb_points-earning.

## Related
- kb_customers
- kb_account-page

---

# Points Earning Issues

## Symptoms
- Customer placed an order but balance shows no point increase.
- Activity log shows no Place Order earning event.

## Common Causes
- Order status does not match the trigger setting (Paid vs Fulfilled).
- Place Order program is restricted by customer type (All members / VIP only / Member & non-member) and the customer doesn't qualify.
- Order paid partially or fully with Store Credit while merchant excluded Store Credit from earning (Free/Essential plans exclude the entire order, not just the credit portion).
- Bug in app.

## Resolution Steps
- **Step 1:** Ask for order number, customer email, current order status. Request Order access if needed.
- **Step 2:** Check **Joy Admin -> App Settings -> Order** and **Place Order -> Reward setting -> Apply for which type** to confirm trigger condition.
- **Step 3:** Compare order status and customer type to the program settings to identify mismatch.
- **Step 4:** If the order was paid partially with Store Credit:
  - On Free/Essential: explain that excluding Store Credit excludes the whole order, no points awarded.
  - On Advanced/Ultimate: recommend using Rule Engine and ticking the "exclude store credit only" option there.
- **Step 5:** If settings and order data match expected behavior but points still missing, escalate to team to investigate and append `<escalate_human>` to the reply.

## Escalation
- Settings match but no points awarded -> escalate to team with order ID, customer email, screenshots, and append `<escalate_human>` to the reply.

## Related
- kb_earning-programs (place order rules)
- kb_rule-engine (advanced exclusion rules)
- kb_pricing (plan-based feature limits)

---

## Symptoms
- Merchant requests retroactive points for old orders.
- Often happens after install, after changing earning rules, or when merchant forgot to switch program to Live mode.

## Resolution Steps
- **Step 1:** Ask merchant why they need the points update — is it to welcome back existing customers, or to retroactively credit points for actual orders?
- **Step 2 (Case A - encourage existing customers to come back):**
  - Suggest adding a fixed bonus (100–200 pts) to all existing customers via CSV adjustment.
  - Recommend sending a newsletter announcing the loyalty program.
  - Review their reward setup and propose engagement programs (signup, follow social, birthday, review, referral).
- **Step 3 (Case B - merchant truly needs points based on actual orders):**
  - Collect from merchant: store URL, date range for the update, and whether VIP tier should also be re-calculated.
  - Tell the merchant our team will run the past-order points update for them.
  - Escalate to team with: store URL, date range, whether VIP tier should also be recalculated. Team will run the past-order points update. Append `<escalate_human>` to the reply. Bot stops here — team handles the rest.
  - For all-time updates, the merchant can use **Settings -> Sync points for past orders** themselves.

## Escalation
- If the update doesn't reflect, escalate to team and append `<escalate_human>` to the reply.

## Related
- kb_points-advanced (sync past orders, dev tools)
- kb_vip-tiers (re-calculation of tiers)

---

## Symptoms
- Program no longer behaves as expected (earning wrong, redeem not applying, milestone not triggering).
- Merchant says they never changed it or doesn't remember the previous setup.

## Resolution Steps
- **Step 1:** To audit a specific program's history, collect the merchant email, store URL, and program name (or Program ID if available).
- **Step 2:** Tell the merchant our team will investigate the program history for them.
- **Step 3:** Escalate to team with the merchant email and program name for investigation. Append `<escalate_human>` to the reply. Bot stops here — team handles the rest.

## Notes
- Supported for any program that has a Program ID: earning, redeeming, perks, exclusive deals, milestones, etc.

## Related
- kb_earning-programs
- kb_redeeming-programs

---

## Decision Points
- **Q1: How is "first order" defined?** Shopify order history, or app's own logic? This decides the whole setup.
- **Q2: Stack with other programs?** If yes, single program with stacking allowed; if no, separate first-order program with highest priority and "Stop giving rewards after this program" enabled.
- **Q3: Existing customers - should past orders be adjusted?** Customers with prior orders won't qualify as first-order; confirm with merchant.

## Resolution Steps - Case A: First Order = Shopify history
- **Step 1:** Use the built-in Shopify segment **"Customers who have purchased at least once"**.
- **Step 2:** In **Shopify Flow**, create a flow:
  - Trigger: Customer joined segment "Customers who have purchased at least once"
  - Action: Add tag (e.g. `ordered_before`)
- **Step 3:** Set up programs:
  - First Order Program: highest priority, Stop giving rewards after this program ON, condition = customer does NOT have tag.
  - Subsequent Orders Program: lower priority, condition = customer HAS tag.
- **Note:** Old customers won't be auto-tagged; merchant may need to bulk-tag manually.

## Signup / welcome bonus points not awarded

**Symptoms:** A customer signed up but the signup/welcome bonus points were not added.

**How Sign up reward works now:** A customer only earns the Sign up reward when **both** are true — (1) they **log in to a customer account**, and (2) the **Joy embed widget is active/enabled** on the storefront. Leaving an email only (signup form, checkout, or newsletter) no longer grants the reward. This applies to all account types.

**Common Causes:**
- The customer only left their email (form/checkout/newsletter) but never logged in to a customer account.
- The **Joy embed widget is disabled** on the theme, so the reward could not trigger.
- The customer did not complete the exact signup action tied to the bonus.
- The program was in **Sandbox**, not live, when they signed up.

**Resolution Steps:**
- **Step 1:** Confirm the signup program is live (not Sandbox), the customer **logged in** with the bonus email, and the **Joy embed widget is enabled** (Theme Editor → App embeds → Joy Loyalty - Widget → Save).
- **Step 2:** If everything checks out, add the points manually via **Customers → adjust points**.
- **Step 3:** For repeated misses, escalate with the affected customer emails.

## Resolution Steps - Case B: First Order = App logic via Shopify Flow
- **Step 1:** In Shopify Flow, trigger on `Order created`, condition `Customer does NOT have tag ordered_before`.
- **Step 2:** Actions: Adjust point/credit (Joy action) + Add tag `ordered_before`.
- **Step 3:** Inside Joy app, programs that should only apply to subsequent orders must have a condition requiring tag `ordered_before`.

## Escalation
- Confirm logic and impact with merchant before triggering. Adjust legacy customer points on request.

## Related
- kb_earning-programs
- kb_integrations-shopify-flow
- kb_rule-engine

---

# Earning Programs

All earning programs live at **Joy Admin → Reward programs → Earning programs → Add rule**.

| Program | Plans | What it rewards |
|---|---|---|
| Place order | All | Points or store credit per purchase |
| Place subscription | All | Recurring subscription orders |
| Sign up | All | Joining the loyalty program — granted only when the customer **logs in** to a customer account **and** the Joy embed widget is **active** on the storefront (leaving an email alone does not qualify; applies to all account types) |
| Sign up newsletters | All | Subscribing to newsletter (Shopify marketing status must be "subscribed", not "pending") |
| Birthday reward | Essential, Advanced, Ultimate | Customer's birthday |
| Member anniversary | Advanced, Ultimate | Days since signup (e.g., 365 = 1 year) |
| Write review | Pro and above | Product reviews via Klaviyo, Air, Judge.me, Loox, Okendo, Tydal, Reviews.io, Stamped, Fera, Yotpo, Growave |
| Social activity | Essential, Advanced, Ultimate | Follows, likes, shares on social platforms |
| Instagram comment / story reply / story mention / live comment | Advanced, Ultimate | Specific Instagram interactions (Business or Creator account required) |
| Google reviews | Essential, Advanced, Ultimate | Reviews on Google Places — admin approves before points granted |
| Fill out a survey | All | Survey completion via Shopify Flow |
| Visit website | All | Customer visits to store |
| Streak bonus challenge | All | Consecutive-day visits with milestone bonuses |
| Custom program | Advanced and above | Custom triggers — visit specific page, custom event, Shopify Flow |
| Submit receipt | Advanced and above | Photo of purchase receipt — admin approves |
| Submit form | Advanced and above | Proof of off-platform actions (TikTok, UGC, etc.) via custom form |

---

## Reward methods

- **Per amount spent**: 1 point per $1
- **One-time at threshold**: 100 points when order ≥ $50
- **Per item quantity**: 5 points per item
- **Fixed per order**: 10 points per order

## Reward type

- **Points** — abstract units, redeemed for discount coupons
- **Store credit** — real $ value, applied at checkout. Joy prompts inline for Shopify permission on first use; one-time grant covers all programs

## Multi-currency earning rates (Advanced, Ultimate)

Toggle **Apply different rates by currency** in Reward settings. Joy reads the **presentment currency** customer paid in. Set rates proportional to FX rates so reward value stays consistent.

## Conditions ("Check if")

**Recipe — earn only on full-price items (exclude sale items):** keep sale items in a dedicated collection (e.g. "SALE"), set the Place-order rule's product condition to **exclude** that collection, and mirror the same restriction on the redeeming program's **Applicable categories** so redemption doesn't stack on discounted items.


Customer / Order / Product / Collection — combine with **All** or **Any** logic. Common use: exclude orders by tag (e.g., orders from non-Shopify platforms).

## Trust-based earning not awarded (visit / social / Google review)

For Visit website, social, Instagram, and Google review programs, the customer must be **logged in** when performing the action. **Google review** points sit in a **pending/approval queue** the merchant must approve before they are granted. **Instagram** needs an IG Business/Creator account connected and the customer's IG username linked.

## Fraud prevention

- Max earning per rule per customer
- Earning frequency limit
- Auto-revoke on cancelled/refunded orders

## Calculation notes

- Shipping and taxes are NOT included by default (toggle "Reward for additional fee" to include them)
- Calculated on order subtotal in primary currency unless multi-currency is on

---

## Birthday reward (Essential+)

- **Date format**: MM/DD/YYYY or DD/MM/YYYY
- **Delivery**: Automatic (system grants) or Manual (customer claims)
- **Timing**: Exact birthday, calendar month, or custom range (X days before/after)
- Optional: grant reward N days before exact birthday
- Customer must have a birthday on file — see kb_birthday for how to collect

## Member anniversary (Advanced+)

Reward N days after customer joined loyalty program. Common: 30 days, 6 months, 365 days. Reward types: Points, Discount %, Discount amount. Customers see/claim from their loyalty page.

---

## Generic social activity (Essential+)

Trust-based — Joy can't verify completion via social APIs. Customer is guided to the URL; most follow through.

## Instagram programs (Advanced, Ultimate)

All require **Instagram Business or Creator account** + connection in **Joy Admin → Integrations → Instagram** (one connection covers all IG programs). Customers must link their Instagram username in the widget.

| Event | Detail |
|---|---|
| Comment on Instagram | Tracks comments on selected posts |
| Story reply | Text replies only — emoji-only reactions filtered out |
| Story mention | Customer mentions your brand in their story (24h window) |
| Live comment | Comments during your IG Live |

Stories require the Instagram mobile app. Detection happens while content is live.

---

## Write review (Pro+)

Connects to your review app: Klaviyo, Air Reviews, Judge.me, Loox, Okendo, Tydal, Reviews.io, Stamped, Fera, Yotpo, Growave. See kb_integrations-reviews for per-app setup.

## Google reviews (Essential+)

- Enter your Google Places URL
- After customer leaves a review, merchant sees it in **Activities** panel
- **Earning points** = approve before granting; **Instant points** = grant immediately

## Fill out a survey (All)

Connects via **Shopify Flow**. Merchant clicks **Create Shopify flow** → flow links survey completion (e.g., Zigpoll) to point reward.

---

## Visit website (All)

Set points per visit + frequency limit (daily/weekly/monthly). Pair with **Visit Website Popup** in Branding for on-site prompt.

## Streak bonus challenge (All)

Enhanced visit program — daily points + bonus on streak completion. Drives habit-forming engagement.

## Custom program (Advanced+)

Triggers beyond defaults: **Visit a page** (URL exact or contains), **Custom events**, **Shopify Flow** triggers. Build your own logic.

## Submit receipt (Advanced+)

Customer uploads receipt photo → staff approves/rejects in admin. Use case: in-store purchases through POS. Anti-cheat: limit submissions per timeframe.

## Submit form (Advanced+)

Customizable proof form for off-platform actions (TikTok hashtag posts, UGC, external reviews). Optional **Instant reward** gives small points on submit before review.

## Related
- kb_redeeming-programs (how customers spend points)
- kb_points-advanced (expiration, refunds, pending, store credit)
- kb_rule-engine (how multiple programs interact)
- kb_integrations-reviews (review app integrations)
- kb_integrations-shopify-flow (custom flow triggers)
- kb_birthday (how to collect customer birthdays)
